title: Pseudopanax platyphyllus

Pseudopanax platyphyllus is a species of flowering plant native to New Zealand. The plant is also known as the Five Finger, due to its distinctive leaf shape. It is a climbing plant with compound leaves that are often found in a variety of habitats.

intro: Pseudopanax platyphyllus, also known as the lancewood or horopito, is a shrub or small tree native to New Zealand, belonging to the family Aristolochiaceae. The lancewood has been used in traditional Maori medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, providing relief from skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory issues. Its bioactive constituents include aristolochic acids, flavonoids, and sesquiterpenes, which have been shown to exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. The lancewood is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
